key_points: Students debate whether to dorm given 30-min commute proximity
On-campus living recommended for first year social bonding
Traffic into Cincinnati a real factor for commuter students
key_quotes: Half an hour isn't super close but it's definitely doable, just keep in mind an 8am class
Depends on traffic going into Cinci from where you're driving from
I know a few people who live within the 30 minute range and commute

key_points: One current freshman said they hadn't had a good experience socially
Recommended for Aussie exchange student seeking party/sports atmosphere
Student transferred to OSU citing missing social vibe at UC
key_quotes: I am a freshman at the University of Cincinnati. I have yet to have a good experience and often find it a struggle to enjoy living while being there
I visited my girlfriend at Auburn University, and felt like that was the college vibe I've been looking for
University of Florida, Arizona State, UCF, NC State, San Diego State, Cincinnati — all have good party atmospheres

No YouTube comments addressed campus food quality
Cannot score reliably without actual student dining data
key_quotes: No dining-specific quotes available in the data
No dining-specific quotes available in the data

key_points: One student openly struggled and considered transferring freshman year
Engineering students hit calculus wall with no clear support path
SAP bureaucracy caused a student to lose enrollment with no warning
key_quotes: I have yet to have a good experience and often find it a struggle to enjoy living while being there
I'm not incredibly sure if engineering is what I actually want to do, and I don't have the grades to transfer in, thanks mostly to calculus
You forgot to show the mental breakdowns, sleep deprivation, slave wages and the occasional to frequent suicidal ideations — all part of the residency experience

key_points: Aid can be generous — full rides and NMF scholarships reported for strong applicants
International students frequently report aid still not enough to make it affordable
One student's best friend 'barely offered any aid' making UC unaffordable
key_quotes: So sad because this is where my best friend wants to go, but they barely offered her any aid so it's not affordable at all
I received just 20k. Now, paying 20k (rough COA) is double the maximum my parents can pay
UC is literally paying you for all the work you did in high school — think of it as getting paid six figures

key_points: Co-op program is UC's strongest academic differentiator — top 3 nationally
Engineering program is 5 years due to co-op, not 4 — plan accordingly
One student 'not impressed with academics at all' their freshman year
key_quotes: UC includes CS as an engineering program, which means you have to take a lot of BS classes that are meaningless to your major
I am a freshman at the University of Cincinnati... I'm not impressed with the academics at all
Cincinnati pretty much invented the co-op program structure over a century ago, and is still considered one of the Top 3 co-op programs

key_points: SAP policy applied retroactively to high school dual-enrollment credits, blindsiding a family
Common App fee waiver portal reportedly broken with no fallback option
Admissions decisions reportedly delayed — portal behavior confusing applicants
key_quotes: We spoke to someone at BG on 12/15 and was informed the hold was there — she is not meeting SAP
It doesn't have a 'No I don't qualify' option and it's a required field — yeah, same problem, we should email the Admissions Office
People on Twitter are saying nothing changed for them too — College is University of Cincinnati BTW

key_points: Campus widely praised for aesthetics — a frequent positive in decision posts
Cincinnati city itself seen as a real asset with urban opportunities
Ohio weather cited as a concrete negative by multiple students
key_quotes: I toured the University of Cincinnati last year — it is a great campus
Bad weather — Is in Ohio — Safety concerns
I like UC, like Clifton and the UC campus, but there is just something about wanting to leave Cincinnati

key_points: Co-op placements averaging $9,000/semester reported in honors programs
Big 4 accounting internship secured freshman year by a UC student
Strong local employer pipeline — UC co-ops open doors even at entry level
key_quotes: I received an internship offer at EY (a big 4 accounting firm) — no matter what college you go to, you can find your own success
Summer internships and co-ops give pay on average $9,000 per semester, paying off any student loans
UC has good connections to local companies with its co-op program — my dad got a job he's been with ever since

key_points: In-state tuition plus co-op income makes UC one of Ohio's best ROI options
Out-of-state sticker price (~$50k/yr for CCM) shocks students who get no aid
Repeatedly beats Dayton, CWRU, and VT on cost-benefit comparisons
key_quotes: At Virginia Tech I'm paying 50k a year while at University of Cincinnati I'm paying 5k
If I go to Dayton I will have to take out about ~$70,000 in loans but if I go to Cincinnati only ~$30,000
The cost of attendance is way too high for my family — it's about 50k per year as OOS

key_points: Strong practical, career-focused school — not a traditional 'college experience' campus
Students who thrive here are motivated by outcomes and ROI, not prestige
Some students feel something is 'missing' socially compared to flagship rivals
key_quotes: I had a 34 ACT and a 4.0 — my final top two choices were the University of Cincinnati and Iowa State. Simply put, not having to pay a fuckton for college is way more valuable
I am a freshman at UC. I have yet to have a good experience and often find it a struggle to enjoy living while being there
Ten years from now you won't give a flying fuck about which school had the prettier campus — you'll be working in your preferred domain

key_points: **Administration:** SAP policy retroactively applied to high school credits, derailing enrollment
**Social Life:** Multiple students transferred or considered leaving citing missing campus vibe
**Safety:** Student's own pros/cons list cited 'safety concerns' about Cincinnati area
key_quotes: She is not meeting SAP — the SAP issue stems from college classes she took during her junior year of high school, which we did not realize would impact her standing
I have yet to have a good experience and often find it a struggle to enjoy living while being there
Safety concerns — Is in Ohio — Bad weather [listed as explicit cons by a prospective student]

key_points: DAAP design program is nationally recognized — worth applying even as a dream school
Honors/LHP program includes guaranteed co-ops and career placements few schools match
NMF scholarship makes UC surprisingly affordable OOS for high-scoring students
key_quotes: I got into the program that only accepts 25 students, and basically gives you a guaranteed career all the while landing you summer internships and co-ops
UC gives a cool NMF scholarship which makes it super affordable even OOS
I love their comm design (graphic design) program — applying to DAAP
